item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bizarre and brilliant, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So this is how they finally contained Jessica Lambert.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I heard rumors that if you zoom in far enough you will eventually find Minecraft pigs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One of my favorites so far hahaha

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Although I knew the actual breed of the pig in the picture, I felt like it was unnecessary to specify it, thinking that just by picture alone you couldn't discern its breed properly.
But thinking about it, thats a stupid argument probably.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would actually support such an addition; I felt that this was a slight plot hole (if you can call it that).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes you are right, I thought about adding a section explaining that toying with root or the hard drive is forbidden, after all the initials SCP stand for secure, contain, protect and not destroy destroy destroy. SCP-4446 would probably be terminated when forcing its deletion, but what's the point in that?

The reason why simple deletion was even tried, is, that it's relativly risk free. It would either be in the trash can or still be encoded somewhere on the hard drive.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Overall, I enjoyed this (except for maybe the test logs), but there's just one little thing my over-analytical computer geek mind found hard to overlook.

SCP-4446 can not be deleted or edited. Any attempt to do so is prompted by an alert, that sufficient administrative rights are needed, even if executed as an admin.

Admin isn't the highest permission level; root is. Also, why not just wipe the hard drive? That doesn't require any permissions at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That is the better variant that only came to my mind a day after posting. I think I might change it to that all together.

The first idea is basically the same except it is not limited to being one pixel big, but rather every pixel itself has the form of a downscaled pig (or other images), which again is made up of downscaled pigs, tinted in different colors so that from afar it looks like a normal picture of a pig

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I too am a touch confused with how the fractal branches operate. Is it that each pixel in the image itself is another image, and that those images themselves, at their smallest component, are further comprised of images etc, etc?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is great for a first SCP. The anomaly is original and interesting and there's some kind of amusing narrative. Great comedy. +1

item.title: f r a c t a l p i g
item.content:encoded:
I had a chuckle.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a cool idea, I enjoyed it. Just a note, I believe the suffix should be -A, not -a.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
not what "landrace" means.

'landrace' is not a specific breed.

'landrace' is 'the informal natural non-deliberately-cultivated breed that is found in a given area'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Look at all the Landrace piggies
Roaming on the range/
And for all the Landrace piggies
Life is getting strange

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the feedback,

my hopes were that the logs 1 to 3 would foreshadow and lead to the conclusion that is log 4, which was meant to be the reason for SCP-4446's existence: What if the pig you raped comes back to haunt you?

Now that I spell it out, it sounds really stupid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wish I could've explained the fractals with a picture.

The lack of an in depth explanation is because it always sounds wrong for a formal file
"Let me now shove in a tutorial about what fractals are"

It's something I should have done better, thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, this is a good idea but needs better execution, there are spelling errors almost every line.

When zooming into SCP-4446 any sliders, numbers or equal information indicating how far zoomed in one currently is, are immediately set to their maximum possible value. These states won't change, unless zooming out again to SCP-4446's original pixel size of 792x495.

SCP-4446 can possibly be zoomed into indefinetly

Surely if zoom sliders are set to maximum you can no longer zoom in. I assume whatever program you are using anomalously allows you to continue zooming but the first part of this is confusing and doesn't add anything.

I don't like the way the test logs have been set up, there's no pacing or escalation from mundane to strange and ending with a dumb joke about having sex with a pig kills it.

-1, but would be promising after some rewriting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is actually a pretty amusing concept, but it's brought down by SPaG errors and a somewhat confusing explanation of the fractals1.

EDIT: Oh, this. This is beautiful.

Footnotes
1. NB: I am a massive idiot, so I might just not be "getting it".

item.title: Oof
item.content:encoded:
Hey, so this is my first SCP, I hope its not too scrappy.
I tried photoshopping an actual pig fractal but the results were really bad oh well^^

Image used:
https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/b/bb/Mei%C3%9Fner_Landschwein_-_Sau%3B_Foto_R._Klemm_%28zugeschnitten%29.jpg

I (hopefully) improved the text a bit according to feedback in the comments. Props to ModernMajorGeneral & Shaggydredlocks
